CREATE TABLE test_drivers (id INT PRIMARY KEY, driver_name VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_drivers (id, driver_name) VALUES (1, 'OpenLink Generic 32 Bit Driver v4.0');
INSERT INTO test_drivers (id, driver_name) VALUES (2, 'OpenLink Oracle Driver v3.1');
INSERT INTO test_drivers (id, driver_name) VALUES (3, 'OpenLink SQL Server Driver v5.2');
SELECT driver_name FROM test_drivers WHERE id = 1;
SELECT driver_name FROM test_drivers WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_drivers WHERE id > 1);
SELECT driver_name FROM test_drivers WHERE driver_name LIKE 'OpenLink%';
SELECT AVG(id) FROM test_drivers WHERE id BETWEEN 1 AND 3;
DELETE FROM test_drivers WHERE driver_name = 'OpenLink Oracle Driver v3.1';
SELECT driver_name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_drivers) AS total_drivers FROM test_drivers;
